files.year_idx_format: int32_t max_okey header (4 bytes) followed by uint8_t[max_okey+1]; 0xFF for unused slots

storage_extensions.usage: Used only during setup to build the lineitem.l_year_idx extension (already done). Not used directly in the query scan loop.

files.year_idx_format: uint8_t[59986052]; entry i = (year - 1992) for the order of lineitem row i (0=1992..6=1998); all entries valid

storage_extensions.usage: mmap year_idx.bin as uint8_t[59986052]. In the scan: l_year_arr[i] replaces okey_year_arr[l_orderkey[i]]. Sequential access (row index i) — no LLC misses, hardware prefetcher works perfectly. Eliminates l_orderkey column from scan entirely.

join_order.notes: Parallel scan of p_name.bin (112MB, stride=56) across all threads. Build shared uint8_t is_green[2000001] (2MB, L3-resident). Pass 1 of lineitem scan: read l_partkey only (240MB), collect qualifying row indices into per-thread vectors (~51K per thread).

Parallel scan of partsupp raw columns (128MB). Parallel CAS-based insertion of ~440K green entries into compact hash (capacity 1048576 = 2^20, key=int64, value=double, 16MB total — L3-resident). No single-threaded bottleneck. Use uint64_t atomic CAS to claim slots; write value after key is claimed. Probe during pass 2 only for qualifying rows.
uint8_t supp_nat[100001] — 100KB, L1-resident. Single-threaded build from s_suppkey + s_nationkey columns (tiny).

join_order.probe: selection vector row indices in pass 2
join_order.notes: mmap uint8_t[59986052]. Sequential access via sorted selection vector indices — hardware prefetcher works. No LLC misses. Replaces random 60MB okey_year_arr lookup.
char nation_names[25][26] from n_name.bin — 650 bytes, L1-resident.

scan.notes: Pass 1: scan l_partkey only (240MB sequential). For each row i where is_green[l_partkey[i]], append i to thread-local selection vector. Pass 2: after merging and sorting the global selection vector (3.3M sorted indices, 13.2MB), iterate in sorted order gathering l_suppkey[idx], l_year_idx[idx], l_extprice[idx], l_discount[idx], l_quantity[idx]. Sorted order ensures ascending cache-line access within each column. l_orderkey is NOT needed — dropped entirely.

data_structures.part_filter: uint8_t is_green[2000001] — 2MB, built in parallel from p_name scan. Shared read-only across threads.
data_structures.compact_ps_hash: Flat hash: uint64_t keys[1048576] (sentinel=0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF, atomically claimed via CAS), double values[1048576]; 16MB total, L3-resident. Parallel CAS-based insertion of 440K entries across all threads. key = (uint64(ps_partkey)<<32)|uint32(ps_suppkey).
data_structures.year_lookup: uint8_t l_year_arr[59986052] — mmap of lineitem.l_year_idx extension. Sequential access during pass 2 (sorted row indices). Hardware prefetcher handles perfectly.
data_structures.selection_vector: std::vector<int32_t> global_selvec (~3.3M entries = 13.2MB). Built by merging per-thread vectors after pass 1. Sorted with std::sort before pass 2.
data_structures.aggregation: double thread_agg[25][7] per thread (1400 bytes, L1-resident, cache-line aligned with alignas(64) padding).

parallelism.notes: Phase 1 (p_name scan + partsupp scan): nthreads parallel for schedule(static). CAS-based parallel hash insertion. Phase 2 (pass 1 scan): morsel-driven over l_partkey (60M rows). Phase 3 (sort selvec): std::sort on merged global vector (single-threaded, 3.3M entries ~3ms). Phase 4 (pass 2 gather+agg): divide sorted selvec evenly across nthreads, each thread processes its slice sequentially (sorted indices ensure ascending cache access). Thread-local agg[25][7] merged at end.

optimization_notes: Iter 1 timing (exclusive per phase): data_loading=52.6ms, dim_filter=6.3ms, build_joins=27.6ms, main_scan=41.7ms. Three root causes: (1) Category C/B — data_loading overhead from MADV_WILLNEED on 2.16GB lineitem data; only l_partkey needs prefetch since payload columns are accessed for only 5.5% of rows; (2) Category B/E — main_scan is bandwidth-limited at ~51GB/s reading 2.16GB of lineitem data for every row, while 94.5% of rows are rejected after l_partkey check; replacing single-pass with two-pass eliminates reading 1.74GB of payload columns for non-qualifying rows (effective pass 2 bandwidth ~96MB for 3.3M qualifying rows); additionally, okey_year_arr random 60MB access causes LLC misses — eliminated by new lineitem.l_year_idx column version (uint8_t[59986052], sequential access, built in 1.7s); (3) Category D — single-threaded partsupp hash insertion of 440K entries (~22ms) replaced with CAS-based parallel insertion across all 64 threads. Expected new totals: data_loading ~5ms, dim_filter ~6ms, build_joins ~4ms, pass1+sort ~6ms, pass2+agg ~8ms, total ~30ms.
